frameworks.name: decoded fMRI neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: closed-loop targeting of specific multivoxel representations via machine-learning decoders

frameworks.name: closed-loop neurofeedback
frameworks.one_line: real-time feedback paradigm with longstanding methodological challenges

frameworks.name: multivoxel pattern analysis
frameworks.one_line: MVPA decoders used to identify and reinforce target neural patterns

frameworks.name: double-blind placebo-controlled experimental design
frameworks.one_line: rigorous trial structure adapted to neurofeedback intervention studies

core_question: How can rigorous decoded fMRI neurofeedback experiments be designed and implemented to address methodological challenges in the field?
key_assumptions: specific multivoxel representations can be targeted via machine-learning decoders
unconscious occurrences of target representations can be reinforced
double-blind placebo-controlled designs are feasible in neurofeedback
interpretations: decoded fMRI neurofeedback can resolve longstanding methodological problems of classical neurofeedback
machine-learning advancements make targeting unconscious multivoxel patterns now feasible
out_of_scope_note: methods/tutorial paper — no empirical manipulations or findings to extract
